acceptance_targets: [ch11_f005] PASS if no more than two of the cited moments rely on explicit "like" simile construction after revision; FAIL if three or more still use the same comparative template in those locations.

[ch11_f006] PASS if at least two of the four cited beats no longer use "Marisol felt..." and still clearly communicate her emotional state through observable behavior or bodily response; FAIL if three or more cited beats retain the same lead-in construction.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two of the cited moments rely on explicit "like" simile construction after revision; FAIL if three or more still use the same comparative template in those locations.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same comparative simile scaffold ("X like Y") more than twice at high-pressure beats, creating detectable formula repetition.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at least three of the cited lines (especially snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:64, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:216, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:355) to deliver force through concrete verbs, texture, and spatial pressure rather than comparative template language.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the four cited beats no longer use "Marisol felt..." and still clearly communicate her emotional state through observable behavior or bodily response; FAIL if three or more cited beats retain the same lead-in construction.
findings.problem: Emotion delivery overuses the "Marisol felt..." construction, which reads as mechanical signaling rather than scene-specific embodiment.
findings.rewrite_direction: At the cited lines, replace at least two "Marisol felt..." turns with action-led or dialogue-adjacent physiological cues that externalize stress without announcing emotion.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When choosing between reputationally safe abstraction and specific discomfort, choose specificity. Render addiction, sexuality, deceit, class shame, and grief at lived scale. In edge cases, preserve character truth and causal accountability over reader comfort or institutional decorum.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene demands bodily, moral, or economic specificity, euphemistic phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with concrete stakes and observable consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; full forms appear only for emphasis, ritual language, or character-specific formal posture

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density and pressure modes: pair high-conflict chapters with quiet domestic or documentary chapters, vary dialogue-to-interiority ratios, and prevent identical chapter skeletons in consecutive sequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: contemporary literary diction with class-aware, borough-specific concreteness; avoid prestige-neutral workshop language
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Marisol, with rare brief distance expansions for institutional scenes
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternating tensile compression and reflective extension; line-level cadence should carry emotional pressure, not just information
